title: Money — canonical encoding
description: `money = { 1 => int, 2 => currency }` (§16.3). Three cases exercise the smallest, a two-byte, and a four-byte shortest-form integer encoding of `minor_units`, plus the fixed-length `currency` text string.

cases.name: typical amount
cases.note: 1999 minor units needs the 2-byte uint form (0x19 + big-endian u16, since 255 < 1999 < 65536): head byte 0x19, then 0x07 0xCF (1999 = 0x07CF). Map has 2 entries -> head 0xA2. Key 1 (0x01) sorts before key 2 (0x02). 'USD' is 3 ASCII bytes -> text-string head 0x63.

cases.name: zero amount
cases.note: 0 is the single-byte uint form (head byte 0x00 itself, no argument bytes) — the shortest-form rule, not a special case for zero.

cases.name: amount requiring 4-byte uint form
cases.note: 100000000 (1e8) exceeds 65535, so it takes the 4-byte form: head byte 0x1A then 4 big-endian bytes (100000000 = 0x05F5E100).
